text: 76. TEA MATTERS In the appeal filed by M/s. Lipton Tea (India) Ltd., the appellant company has brought in challenge the order of the High Court of judicature at Patna in Writ Petition No.1027 of 1977 which was disposed of along with other cognate matters by a common judgment. The appellant had contended before the High Court that the Market Act cannot apply to the transaction of manufactured blended tea sold in packed tins and packets by it in the State of Bihar, consisting of areas of different market committees. According to the appellant, the object of the Market Act was to provide for better regulation of buying and selling of agricultural produce. It was for the benefit of the agriculturists by providing them a market assuring a reasonable price of their products and also eliminating unhealthy competition and loss due to malpractices prevailing in the market. That the appellant was neither an agriculturist nor did it purchase any article from any agriculturist in the Bihar State. That it purchased tea in auction under the Tea Act held at various notified centres in other States outside the Bihar territory. That the purchased tea was blended at appellant's factories which were also situated outside Bihar. Only after the purchased tea had undergone manufacturing process in appellant's tea factories, after blending and preparation of appropriate final product packed in tins and other receptacles, this marketable commodity "tea" consisting of red label, green label tea etc. was being brought for sale within the territories of the State of Bihar.
